questions.q: Explain the concept of atmospheric circulation and its significance in distributing heat around the globe. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Atmospheric circulation refers to the movement of air in the atmosphere. It plays a vital role in distributing heat around the globe, leading to various weather phenomena. The uneven heating of the Earth's surface by the sun creates a temperature gradient, which drives atmospheric circulation. The circulation of air helps to distribute heat from the equator towards the poles, resulting in different climate zones.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of atmospheric circulation (1 mark)
Discuss the distribution of heat (2 marks)
Describe the different climate zones (1 mark)

questions.q: Describe the different types of wind patterns and their characteristics. (5 marks)
questions.answer: There are several types of wind patterns, including trade winds, westerlies, and jet streams. Trade winds are gentle winds that blow from the northeast in the Northern Hemisphere and from the southeast in the Southern Hemisphere. Westerlies are mid-latitude winds that blow from the west. Jet streams are fast-moving winds that occur in the upper atmosphere.
marking_scheme: Compare the characteristics of each type (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the concept of high and low-pressure systems and their effects on weather. (5 marks)
questions.answer: High-pressure systems are areas of high atmospheric pressure, while low-pressure systems are areas of low atmospheric pressure. High-pressure systems are associated with fair weather, while low-pressure systems are associated with precipitation and stormy weather. The movement of high and low-pressure systems across the globe leads to various weather phenomena.
marking_scheme: Discuss their effects on weather (2 marks)

questions.q: A ship at sea has a wind speed of 60 km/h. If the density of air is 1.2 kg/m³, calculate the pressure gradient force acting on the ship. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Pressure gradient force (P) = density (ρ) x wind speed (v)² / (2 x g)
ho = 1.2 kg/m³, v = 60 km/h = 60 x (5/18) m/s = 16.67 m/s, g = 9.8 m/s²
P = 1.2 x (16.67)² / (2 x 9.8) = 2.5 N/m²
marking_scheme: Calculate the wind speed in m/s (1 mark)
Apply the formula for pressure gradient force (2 marks)

questions.q: A low-pressure system has a central pressure of 990 mbar and a pressure gradient of 0.01 mbar/m. If the radius of the low-pressure system is 500 km, calculate the total pressure gradient force acting on a 10 km² area. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Total pressure gradient force (F) = pressure gradient (dp/dx) x area (A) x radius (r)
dp/dx = 0.01 mbar/m, A = 10 km² = 10 x 10^6 m², r = 500 km = 500 x 10^3 m
First, convert mbar to Pascals: 1 mbar = 100 Pa
F = (0.01 x 100) x 10 x 10^6 x 500 x 10^3 / (2 x 9.8)
F = 1.27 x 10^8 N
marking_scheme: Apply the formula for total pressure gradient force (2 marks)

questions.q: If the temperature at the equator is 35°C and at the poles is -50°C, and the distance between the equator and the poles is 10,000 km, calculate the temperature gradient. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Temperature gradient (dT/dx) = ΔT / Δx
ΔT = 35°C - (-50°C) = 85°C = 85 K, Δx = 10,000 km = 10,000 x 10^3 m
First, convert temperature from Celsius to Kelvin: ΔT = 85 K
dT/dx = 85 / (10,000 x 10^3) = 0.0085 K/m
marking_scheme: Convert temperature from Celsius to Kelvin (1 mark)
Apply the formula for temperature gradient (2 marks)
